snp2.1:1.4: & listen intently to what I say.
snp2.1:2.1: Thus, spirits, you should all be attentive.
snp2.1:2.2: Show goodwill to the human race.
snp2.1:3.2: whatever exquisite treasure in the heavens,
snp2.1:3.3: does not, for us, equal the Tathāgata.
snp2.1:3.4: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Buddha.
snp2.1:3.5: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:4.2: discovered by the Sakyan Sage in concentration:
snp2.1:4.3: There is nothing to equal that Dhamma.
snp2.1:4.4: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Dhamma.
snp2.1:4.5: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:5.1: What the excellent Awakened One extolled as pure
snp2.1:5.2: and called the concentration of unmediated knowing:
snp2.1:5.3: No equal to that concentration can be found.
snp2.1:5.4: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Dhamma.
snp2.1:5.5: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:6.1: The eight persons—the four pairs— praised by those at peace:
snp2.1:6.3: They, disciples of the One Well-Gone, deserve offerings.
snp2.1:6.4: What is given to them bears great fruit.
snp2.1:6.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:6.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:7.3: on attaining their goal, plunge into the deathless,
snp2.1:7.4: freely enjoying the liberation they’ve gained.
snp2.1:7.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:7.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:8.1: An Indra pillar, planted in the earth,
snp2.1:8.2: that even the four winds cannot shake:
snp2.1:8.3: That, I tell you, is like the person of integrity,
snp2.1:8.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:8.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:9.1: Those who have seen clearly the noble truths
snp2.1:9.2: well-taught by the one deeply discerning—
snp2.1:9.3: regardless of what [later] might make them heedless—
snp2.1:9.4: will come to no eighth state of becoming,
snp2.1:9.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:9.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:10.1: At the moment of attaining sight,
snp2.1:11.1: One is completely released from the four states of deprivation,
snp2.1:11.2: and incapable of committing the six great wrongs.
snp2.1:11.3: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:11.4: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:12.1: Whatever bad deed one may do
snp2.1:12.4: an incapability ascribed to one who has seen the Way.
snp2.1:12.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:12.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:13.1: Like a forest grove with flowering tops
snp2.1:13.2: in the first month of the heat of the summer,
snp2.1:13.3: so is the foremost Dhamma he taught,
snp2.1:13.4: for the highest benefit, leading to unbinding.
snp2.1:13.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Buddha.
snp2.1:13.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:14.2: unsurpassed, he taught the foremost Dhamma.
snp2.1:14.3: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Buddha.
snp2.1:14.4: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:15.1: Ended the old, there is no new taking birth.
snp2.1:15.2: Dispassioned their minds toward future becoming,
snp2.1:15.3: they, with no seed, no desire for growth,
snp2.1:15.4: enlightened, go out like this flame.
snp2.1:15.5: This, too, is an exquisite treasure in the Saṅgha.
snp2.1:15.6: By this truth may there be well-being.
snp2.1:16.2: —on the earth, in the sky— let us pay homage to the Buddha,
snp2.1:16.3: the Tathāgata worshipped by beings human & divine.
snp2.1:17.2: —on the earth, in the sky— let us pay homage to the Dhamma
snp2.1:17.3: & the Tathāgata worshipped by beings human & divine.
snp2.1:18.2: —on the earth, in the sky— let us pay homage to the Saṅgha
snp2.1:18.3: & the Tathāgata worshipped by beings human & divine.
